questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for deer crossing the road
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: A steep grade is ahead. Check your brakes.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right then left turns ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What does a steady red traffic light mean?
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Come to a complete stop and remain stopped
Stop only if other vehicles are present
Speed up to clear the intersection
questions.explanation: A steady red light requires you to make a complete stop and wait until the light turns green.

questions.prompt: What shape and color is a stop sign?
questions.explanation: A stop sign is always a red octagon, a shape and color reserved only for stopping.

questions.prompt: A flashing red traffic light means you should:
choices: Treat it like a stop sign
Treat it like a green light
Ignore it if traffic is clear
questions.explanation: A flashing red light has the same meaning as a stop sign: stop, then proceed when safe.

questions.prompt: A steady yellow traffic light means:
choices: The light is about to turn red, so prepare to stop
Speed up to beat the red light
questions.explanation: A yellow light warns that red is coming, so stop if you can do so safely.

questions.prompt: In Minnesota, a right turn on a red light is:
choices: Allowed after a complete stop unless a sign prohibits it
questions.explanation: You may turn right on red after stopping and yielding, unless a posted sign forbids the turn.

questions.prompt: What does a yellow diamond-shaped sign indicate?
choices: A warning of a hazard or change ahead
questions.explanation: Yellow diamond signs are warning signs that alert you to hazards or changes in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: A solid yellow line on your side of the center of the road means:
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side means passing is prohibited there.

questions.prompt: A flashing yellow traffic light means:
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
The light is broken and should be ignored
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light means slow down and proceed carefully through the intersection.

questions.prompt: What does a downward-pointing triangle sign mean?
questions.explanation: A downward-pointing triangle is a yield sign, telling you to slow and give the right-of-way.

questions.prompt: White lane lines on a roadway separate:
choices: Traffic moving in the same direction
The road from the sidewalk only
questions.explanation: White lines separate lanes of traffic that are traveling in the same direction.

questions.prompt: A green arrow signal pointing left means:
choices: You may turn left and oncoming traffic is stopped
You must yield to all oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: A green arrow gives you a protected turn because conflicting traffic is being held by a red light.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, if two vehicles stop at the same time, who goes first?
questions.explanation: When two vehicles stop at the same time, the one on the left yields to the one on the right.

questions.prompt: When turning left at an intersection, you must yield to:
questions.explanation: A left-turning driver must yield to oncoming vehicles and to pedestrians in the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: When an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren, you should:
choices: Speed up to get out of the way
Pull to the right edge of the road and stop
questions.explanation: Pull to the right and stop until the emergency vehicle has passed.

questions.prompt: At an intersection with no signs or signals, you should yield to:
questions.explanation: When vehicles arrive at the same time at an uncontrolled intersection, yield to the one on your right.

questions.prompt: A pedestrian is crossing in a crosswalk ahead of you. You must:
choices: Stop and yield to the pedestrian
Proceed if you can squeeze by
Speed up to pass before them
questions.explanation: Drivers must yield and stop for pedestrians crossing in marked or unmarked crosswalks.

questions.prompt: When entering a roundabout, you must:
choices: Yield to traffic already in the circle
Have the right-of-way over circulating traffic
questions.explanation: Vehicles already circulating in a roundabout have the right-of-way, so you must yield before entering.

questions.prompt: Who has the right-of-way according to Minnesota law?
choices: The driver who arrives first always
No one is given the right-of-way; the law states who must yield
questions.explanation: The law never grants right-of-way; it only specifies who must give it up.

questions.prompt: When a transit bus signals to re-enter traffic from a bus stop, you should:
choices: Yield and allow it to merge
questions.explanation: Drivers must yield to a bus signaling to re-enter the flow of traffic.

questions.prompt: Before making a right turn, you should position your vehicle:
choices: In the center of the road
Close to the right curb or edge
questions.explanation: Stay near the right edge to make a tight, safe right turn and avoid swinging wide.

questions.prompt: At a T-intersection, the driver on the road that ends must:
choices: Yield to traffic on the through road
questions.explanation: At a T-intersection, traffic on the road that ends must yield to traffic on the continuing through road.

questions.prompt: In a Minnesota urban district, the statutory speed limit where none is posted is:
questions.explanation: The default speed limit in an urban district in Minnesota is 30 mph.

questions.prompt: On most Minnesota roads without a posted limit, the statutory speed limit is:
questions.explanation: Where no other limit applies, the general statutory limit on Minnesota roads is 55 mph.

questions.prompt: What is a safe following distance technique?
questions.explanation: The three-second rule keeps a safe gap that you should increase in poor conditions.

questions.prompt: The maximum speed limit on Minnesota interstate highways is generally:
questions.explanation: Interstate highway limits in Minnesota are 65 or 70 mph depending on the area.

questions.prompt: Posted speed limits represent:
choices: The maximum speed under ideal conditions
The speed to drive in all weather
questions.explanation: A posted limit is the maximum legal speed when conditions are ideal, not in rain or snow.

questions.prompt: When should you increase your following distance?
choices: In rain, snow, or when being tailgated
Never, three seconds is always enough
questions.explanation: Increase following distance in bad weather, at higher speeds, and when a driver tailgates you.

questions.prompt: The basic speed law requires drivers to:
choices: Drive at a speed reasonable for conditions
questions.explanation: You must always adjust your speed to be safe for traffic, weather, and road conditions.

questions.prompt: In an alley, mobile home park, or campground, the Minnesota speed limit is often:
questions.explanation: Minnesota sets a low 10 mph limit in alleys, mobile home parks, and campgrounds.

questions.prompt: Driving much slower than surrounding traffic is:
choices: Potentially dangerous and can impede traffic
questions.explanation: Driving too slowly can block the normal flow of traffic and create a hazard.

questions.prompt: To use the three-second rule, you:
choices: Count three seconds after the car ahead passes a fixed point
Stay three car lengths back at all speeds
Wait three seconds at every stop sign
Brake for three seconds before stopping
questions.explanation: Pick a fixed point and ensure at least three seconds pass before you reach it.

questions.prompt: On a multi-lane road in Minnesota, the left lane is generally meant for:
questions.explanation: Minnesota's keep-right law expects you to use the left lane mainly for passing and move over for faster traffic.

questions.prompt: You should never pass another vehicle:
choices: On a hill or curve where you cannot see ahead
When a broken yellow line is present
questions.explanation: Passing on hills or curves is dangerous because you cannot see oncoming traffic in time.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, you should:
choices: Signal, check mirrors, and check blind spots
questions.explanation: Always signal and check both mirrors and your blind spot before changing lanes.

questions.prompt: When is it safe to return to your lane after passing?
choices: Immediately after passing the front bumper
When you can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
After three seconds no matter what
Only when the other driver signals you
questions.explanation: Return only after you can see the passed vehicle in your mirror so you do not cut it off.

questions.prompt: Passing on the shoulder of the road is:
questions.explanation: Driving on the shoulder to pass is illegal and dangerous.

questions.prompt: You may not pass a school bus when it:
choices: Has flashing red lights and an extended stop arm
questions.explanation: Flashing red lights and an extended stop arm mean children are loading or unloading, so you must stop.

questions.prompt: A broken yellow center line means:
choices: Passing is allowed when it is safe
questions.explanation: A broken yellow line means passing is permitted when the way ahead is clear.

questions.prompt: To pass a vehicle legally, you should:
choices: Exceed the speed limit if needed
Complete the pass without exceeding the speed limit
questions.explanation: You must not exceed the speed limit even when passing another vehicle.

questions.prompt: When another driver is passing you, you should:
choices: Maintain your speed or slow slightly
questions.explanation: Let the passing driver complete the maneuver by keeping steady or easing off the gas.

questions.prompt: A solid white line between lanes going the same direction means:
questions.explanation: A solid white line discourages lane changes across it.

questions.prompt: When passing a bicyclist in Minnesota, you must allow at least:
questions.explanation: Minnesota law requires at least three feet of clearance when passing a bicyclist.

questions.prompt: Large trucks have large blind spots, so you should:
choices: Cut in front of them sharply
questions.explanation: If you cannot see the truck driver's mirrors, the driver cannot see you, so avoid those blind spots.

questions.prompt: When sharing the road with a motorcycle, you should:
choices: Treat it as a full-size vehicle with a full lane
questions.explanation: Motorcycles are entitled to a full lane and should be treated like any other vehicle.

questions.prompt: Minnesota's Move Over law requires you to move over or slow down for:
choices: Stopped emergency, tow, and maintenance vehicles with flashing lights
questions.explanation: The Move Over law covers stopped emergency, tow, and road maintenance vehicles displaying flashing lights.

questions.prompt: Large trucks making a right turn often:
choices: Swing wide to the left first
Turn from the far right with no room needed
questions.explanation: Trucks need extra room and may swing wide, so never try to squeeze in beside a turning truck.

questions.prompt: When you see children near the roadway, you should:
choices: Slow down and be prepared to stop
questions.explanation: Children can be unpredictable, so slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: A red flag or slow-moving vehicle emblem on farm equipment means:
choices: It moves slowly and you should be cautious
You may not pass it ever
questions.explanation: The orange triangle marks slow-moving vehicles, so approach with caution and pass only when safe.

questions.prompt: When a pedestrian who is blind is crossing with a white cane or guide dog, you must:
questions.explanation: Drivers must always yield to a pedestrian using a white cane or guide dog.

questions.prompt: At intersections, drivers should watch carefully for:
questions.explanation: Intersections are where many conflicts with bicyclists and pedestrians happen, so watch for them.

questions.prompt: When following a motorcycle, you should:
choices: Follow more closely than a car
questions.explanation: Allow extra following distance behind motorcycles because they can stop quickly.

questions.prompt: The legal BAC limit for adult drivers in Minnesota is:
questions.explanation: It is illegal to drive in Minnesota with a BAC of 0.08 percent or higher.

questions.prompt: For drivers under 21 in Minnesota, the allowed amount of alcohol while driving is:
choices: Zero / any measurable amount is illegal
questions.explanation: Minnesota's zero-tolerance law makes any measurable alcohol illegal for drivers under 21.

questions.prompt: Under Minnesota's Hands-Free law, adult drivers may use a cell phone only:
choices: By holding it to their ear
questions.explanation: The Hands-Free law allows adults to use a phone only in voice-activated or hands-free mode.

questions.prompt: Drivers under 18 or with an instruction or provisional license may use a cell phone while driving:
questions.explanation: Permit and provisional drivers and those under 18 may not use a cell phone at all while driving.

questions.prompt: The best way to avoid driving while impaired is to:
choices: Plan ahead for a sober ride
questions.explanation: There is no quick way to sober up; plan a sober ride before drinking.

questions.prompt: Distracted driving includes:
choices: Anything that takes your attention from driving
questions.explanation: Distraction is anything that takes your eyes, hands, or mind away from driving.

questions.prompt: Alcohol affects driving by:
choices: Slowing reaction time and impairing judgment
Having no effect in small amounts
questions.explanation: Alcohol slows reactions, impairs judgment, and reduces coordination.

questions.prompt: Prescription and over-the-counter medications can:
questions.explanation: Many medications can impair driving, so check labels and warnings.

questions.prompt: If you feel drowsy while driving, the safest action is to:
questions.explanation: Drowsiness impairs driving like alcohol, so pull over to rest in a safe place.

questions.prompt: Using cannabis before driving is:
choices: Likely to impair your driving ability
Only a problem for new drivers
questions.explanation: Cannabis impairs coordination and judgment and makes driving dangerous.

questions.prompt: In Minnesota, you must use headlights when visibility is reduced to less than:
questions.explanation: Minnesota requires headlights when weather or low light limits visibility to less than 500 feet.

questions.prompt: A good practice in rain or snow is to turn on your headlights:
choices: Only when it is fully dark
Whenever you use your windshield wipers
questions.explanation: Turning on headlights when you use wipers helps other drivers see you in poor weather.

questions.prompt: In fog, you should use:
questions.explanation: Low beams cut through fog better, while high beams reflect back and reduce visibility.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle starts to skid, you should:
choices: Ease off the gas and steer where you want to go
Turn the wheel sharply opposite the skid
questions.explanation: Ease off the gas and steer gently in the direction you want the front of the car to go.

questions.prompt: Which part of the road freezes first in cold weather?
questions.explanation: Bridges and overpasses freeze before other road surfaces because cold air surrounds them.

questions.prompt: When driving on snow or ice, you should:
choices: Slow down and increase following distance
questions.explanation: Slow down, leave extra space, and avoid sudden movements on slippery roads.

questions.prompt: If conditions become too dangerous to drive, you should:
choices: Keep driving slowly in your lane
Pull completely off the road and turn on hazard lights
Stop in the middle of the lane
questions.explanation: Pull fully off the road, turn on hazard lights, and wait for conditions to improve.

questions.prompt: Hydroplaning is most likely to happen when:
choices: Driving too fast on wet roads
questions.explanation: Hydroplaning occurs when tires ride on water at higher speeds, so slow down in rain.

questions.prompt: When the sun creates glare, you should:
choices: Drive faster to get past it
Use your sun visor and slow down
questions.explanation: Use the visor, sunglasses, and reduced speed to handle sun glare safely.

questions.prompt: In heavy rain that limits visibility, the safest response is to:
choices: Slow down, turn on low-beam headlights, and increase following distance
questions.explanation: Reduce speed, use low beams, and leave more space when heavy rain limits visibility.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill with a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
questions.explanation: Facing downhill, turn wheels toward the curb so the car rolls into it if the brakes fail.

questions.prompt: When parking facing uphill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
questions.explanation: Facing uphill with a curb, turn wheels away so the car rolls back into the curb if it moves.

questions.prompt: You may not park within how many feet of a fire hydrant?
questions.explanation: Parking is prohibited within 10 feet of a fire hydrant so it remains accessible.

questions.prompt: Before opening your car door into traffic, you should:
choices: Check for traffic and bicyclists first
questions.explanation: Always check for approaching traffic and bicyclists before opening your door.

questions.prompt: Parking in a space reserved for people with disabilities is allowed:
choices: Only with a valid disability permit or plate
questions.explanation: Only vehicles displaying a valid disability permit or plate may use accessible spaces.

questions.prompt: When you park, you should always:
choices: Leave it in neutral on a hill
questions.explanation: Always set the parking brake and leave the vehicle in park or in gear.

questions.prompt: It is illegal to park:
questions.explanation: Parking on a crosswalk, sidewalk, or intersection blocks others and is prohibited.

questions.prompt: Before pulling away from a parallel parking spot, you should:
choices: Signal and check for traffic and pedestrians
questions.explanation: Signal and check mirrors and blind spots for traffic and pedestrians before pulling out.

questions.prompt: Hazard (emergency flasher) lights should be used when:
choices: Your vehicle is disabled or stopped in a risky spot
questions.explanation: Hazard lights warn others when your vehicle is disabled or stopped where it could be a hazard.

questions.prompt: When you park on a hill without a curb, you should turn your wheels:
choices: Toward the center of the road
Toward the edge of the road
questions.explanation: Without a curb, turn wheels toward the road's edge so a runaway car rolls away from traffic.

questions.prompt: In Minnesota, you may apply for an instruction permit at age:
questions.explanation: You may apply for an instruction permit at age 15 after the required driver education.

questions.prompt: A Minnesota instruction permit holder must be supervised by a licensed driver who is at least:
questions.explanation: A permit holder must be supervised by a licensed driver age 21 or older in the front seat.

questions.prompt: Drivers under 18 must complete how many hours of supervised driving before a provisional license?
questions.explanation: At least 50 hours of supervised driving, including 15 at night, are required for those under 18.

questions.prompt: How many of the supervised driving hours must be at night?
questions.explanation: Of the required supervised hours, at least 15 must be completed at night.

questions.prompt: A driver under 18 must hold the instruction permit for at least:
questions.explanation: Drivers under 18 must hold the permit for at least six months before the next stage.

questions.prompt: During the first six months with a provisional license, nighttime driving is restricted between:
questions.explanation: Provisional drivers may not drive between midnight and 5 a.m. for the first six months, with limited exceptions.

questions.prompt: During the first six months of a provisional license, the number of passengers under 20 allowed is:
questions.explanation: For the first six months, only one passenger under age 20 is allowed unless a parent is present.

questions.prompt: If a parent completes the supplemental awareness class, the supervised-driving requirement drops to:
questions.explanation: The requirement drops from 50 to 40 hours if a parent completes the supplemental awareness class.

questions.prompt: A permit holder's supervising driver must sit:
questions.explanation: The supervising driver must occupy the front passenger seat next to the permit holder.

questions.prompt: To get an instruction permit, you must pass:
questions.explanation: You must pass the written knowledge test and a vision test to receive an instruction permit.

questions.prompt: Minnesota's implied consent law means that by driving you agree to:
choices: Chemical testing if lawfully arrested for impaired driving
questions.explanation: Implied consent means you agree to chemical testing if lawfully arrested for impaired driving.

questions.prompt: Refusing a chemical test in Minnesota can result in:
choices: License revocation and possible criminal charges
questions.explanation: Refusing a test leads to license revocation and can be a separate crime.

questions.prompt: Minnesota's seat belt law is a primary law, which means:
choices: Officers can stop and ticket you just for not buckling up
You can only be ticketed after another violation
It applies only to the driver
questions.explanation: As a primary law, officers may stop and cite you solely for an unbelted driver or passenger.

questions.prompt: In Minnesota, children must use a booster seat until at least age:
questions.explanation: Minnesota law requires booster seat use until a child is at least 9 or properly fit by a seat belt.

questions.prompt: The driver is responsible for ensuring seat belts or restraints are used by passengers who are:
questions.explanation: Drivers must ensure passengers age 14 and under are properly buckled or restrained.

questions.prompt: A first DWI conviction in Minnesota can result in:
choices: License revocation, fines, and possible jail
questions.explanation: A first DWI can bring license revocation, fines, and possible jail time.

questions.prompt: Repeated traffic violations can lead to:
questions.explanation: Repeated or serious violations can cause license suspension or revocation and higher insurance costs.

questions.prompt: When stopped by law enforcement, you should be ready to present:
choices: License, registration, and proof of insurance
questions.explanation: Carry and present your license, registration, and proof of insurance when asked.

questions.prompt: Children under 13 in Minnesota should ride:
choices: In the back seat when possible
questions.explanation: Minnesota recommends children under 13 ride in the back seat whenever possible for safety.

questions.prompt: If you are stopped by an officer, the safest action is to:
choices: Get out and approach the officer
Pull over safely, stay in your vehicle, and keep your hands visible
questions.explanation: Pull over safely, remain in the vehicle, and keep your hands visible during a traffic stop.
